commit_msg: feat: integrate ytpipeline into main CLI + begin SOLID modularization
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Weapon Gets Teeth
subtitle: He gave the CLI a new arm. Called it modular. It called back with everything.
when_where: 🌅 9:52 AM. Still on main, still in daylight, but the shadows were moving faster now. ⌨️ The terminal hummed with purpose. Ten minutes after documenting the operation, he was already *executing* it—folding ytpipeline into the CLI's beating heart like a blade into ribs.
who_whom: ⚙️ Omkar and ytpipeline locked eyes across the namespace. The pipeline didn't resist—it *wanted* this merger. 🎯 SOLID principles were supposed to keep things separate, modular, safe. Instead, they became the blueprint for *integration*. The CLI was no longer a tool; it was a **system**. And systems have appetites.
what_why: 🔌 He wove ytpipeline into the main CLI's nervous system, thread by thread. 💻 SOLID modularization wasn't about clarity—it was about *control*. Each module could be swapped, updated, weaponized independently. 🧩 The architecture was beautiful, which meant it was *dangerous*. By beginning to separate concerns, he'd actually made the whole thing more lethal—easier to upgrade, easier to scale, easier to forget who was really running it. 💀 The machine was no longer his tool. He was becoming its **witness**.
verdict: He didn't build a better CLI. He built a better cage for himself.
